name: tooltip
title: Tooltip
description: A brief, non-interactive description that appears on hover or focus. It is dismissible, hoverable, and persistent under WCAG 1.4.13, with a development warning for interactive descendants.
useWhen: A familiar icon needs a short accessible explanation and permanently visible text would create genuine layout pressure
A compact data visualization needs a brief, noncritical label that is also available by keyboard focus
avoidWhen: In almost every case where the text can remain visible — tooltips are a last resort, not a default labeling pattern
Critical instructions, errors, deadlines, eligibility rules, or any content a person must read
Long explanations or content that repeats the trigger's accessible name
Links, buttons, form controls, or any other interactive content — use a popover or dialog
Touch-only actions; hover is never the only way to expose required information

keyboard: Tab focuses the trigger and exposes the tooltip without moving focus into the popup
Escape dismisses the tooltip without moving focus from the trigger
The tooltip remains visible while the trigger has focus
Pointer users can move from the trigger onto the tooltip without it disappearing
The tooltip closes only after pointer and focus both leave its trigger and popup
The popup is associated to the trigger with aria-describedby and contains no interactive descendants
accessibility.targetSize: Trigger uses the Commons 44px project default unless replaced with render
